medical_history.chief_complaint: skin fragility and blistering of the face, upper back, and dorsum of hands
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Symptoms were worse during summer, but not affected by her menstrual cycle. At presentation she was pregnant and had undergone relapse of classical childhood CD, controlled between the ages of 10 months and 18 years by a gluten-free diet. From that age she had taken a normal diet. Previous history included a single episode of unexplained acute abdominal pain accompanied by aphasia, lasting several hours but resolving spontaneously, and the removal of a normal appendix without postoperative complications.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Classical childhood CD, controlled between the ages of 10 months and 18 years by a gluten-free diet. Previous history included a single episode of unexplained acute abdominal pain accompanied by aphasia, lasting several hours but resolving spontaneously, and the removal of a normal appendix without postoperative complications.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Skin biopsy revealed no IgA deposition at the dermoepidermal junction. It did, however, show features typical of porphyria with cutaneous lesions. A duodenal biopsy showed near-total villous atrophy with crypt hyperplasia and increased numbers of inflammatory cells in the lamina propria and of intraepithelial lymphocytes, ie., changes typical of CD.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Characteristic emission maximum in the 624- to 628-nm region

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: Antigliadin and reticulin IgA and IgG titers

laboratory_examinations.date: Two years following reinstitution of a gluten-free diet

laboratory_examinations.findings: 5-Aminolevulinic acid (mmol/24h): 103 (reference range: <46), Porphobilinogen (mmol/24h): 26 (reference range: <9), Total porphyrin (nmol/24h): 1730 (reference range: <410)
Total coproporphyrin (nmol/g dry stool): 155 (reference range: <46), Coproporphyrin-III (% of total): 85 (reference range: 17–37), Protoporphyrin (nmol/g dry stool): 480 (reference range: <134)
Antigliadin IgA: neg, IgG: neg; Antireticulin IgA: neg, IgG: neg; Antiendomysium IgA: pos

other_tests.findings: No IgA deposition at the dermoepidermal junction; features typical of porphyria with cutaneous lesions
Near-total villous atrophy with crypt hyperplasia and increased numbers of inflammatory cells in the lamina propria and of intraepithelial lymphocytes, ie., changes typical of CD

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis of variegate porphyria was made based on blood, urine, and fecal analysis showing raised urine and fecal porphyrins, and plasma fluorescence emission spectrometry showing a characteristic emission maximum in the 624- to 628-nm region. Dermatitis herpetiformis was initially considered but ruled out when a skin biopsy revealed no IgA deposition at the dermoepidermal junction, instead showing features typical of porphyria with cutaneous lesions.
